items.briefing:
Australian cyber experts are warning of a growing risk from third-party suppliers, urging organisations to review who has access to their systems and data. Separately, Australians are being targeted by scammers during major life events such as buying a home, having a baby, or retiring — times when people are distracted and more vulnerable to fraud. A new study also found many Australian office workers are ignoring their organisation's AI usage policies, which could expose sensitive data.

A new ransomware strain using artificial intelligence to automate attacks has also been identified globally, raising the threat level for all sectors. **Practical tip:** Remind staff never to enter work or client information into personal or unapproved AI tools.

featured.title: Australian cyber experts warn of third-party risk surge
featured.quote: Weak vendor oversight is leaving Australian schools and businesses exposed to breaches through third parties.
